title: Locate Index in Sorted Array
description:
Given Search for a target value in a sorted array of distinct integers. Return its index if present, otherwise return -1. Use iterative binary search with careful mid computation and bounds updates.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i], target ≤ 10^9
- All elements are distinct
explanation:
Intuition
Binary search repeatedly halves the search interval by comparing the middle element to the target. If the middle is too small we discard the left half, otherwise we discard the right half. This guarantees logarithmic time on sorted arrays.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize left at 0 and right at n-1.
2. Main Algorithm: While left ≤ right, check the middle value and move the boundary that cannot contain the target.
3. Return Result: Return the found index or -1 when the window becomes empty.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(log n) because the search space halves at each step.
- Space Complexity: O(1) since only a few variables are maintained.

Key Insights
- Mid recomputed each loop keeps the invariant correct
- Loop ends when the interval is empty

Common Pitfalls
- Using (left+right)//2 avoids overflow in Python but not in other languages
- Forgetting equal case can result in an infinite loop
hints: Maintain left and right as inclusive bounds
Compare target to the middle element
Shrink the half that cannot contain the target
